meta.title: Judicial Sex-Leveling & The Decline of VMI

questions.question: What was the historical core mission of the Virginia Military Institute (VMI) as described in the lecture?
options: To provide generic liberal arts education to both sexes
To cultivate manly honor, martial valor, and male leadership
To train civilian bureaucrats for state government
To experiment with progressive coeducational pedagogy
questions.answer: To cultivate manly honor, martial valor, and male leadership

questions.question: Which Supreme Court Justice wrote the majority opinion in United States v. Virginia, the case that required VMI to admit women?
options: Antonin Scalia
Clarence Thomas
Ruth Bader Ginsburg
William Rehnquist

questions.question: According to Jeff Schaefer’s explanation, what is the main problem with treating “equality” as a free‑floating legal standard detached from the 14th Amendment’s text?
options: It makes all criminal law unconstitutional
It becomes an empty command that leaves judges with untethered discretion
It prevents states from passing any new legislation
It forces Congress to rewrite the Constitution
questions.answer: It becomes an empty command that leaves judges with untethered discretion

questions.question: How does Aristotle’s definition of equality, as cited in the lecture, differ from the way the modern Supreme Court often reasons about equality?
options: Aristotle insists all people are exactly the same, while the Court emphasizes differences
Aristotle focuses on proportional treatment of like and unlike things, while the Court tends to treat any classification as suspect in itself
Aristotle rejects any legal distinctions, whereas the Court endorses them
Aristotle is concerned only with economic equality, while the Court is concerned only with political equality
questions.answer: Aristotle focuses on proportional treatment of like and unlike things, while the Court tends to treat any classification as suspect in itself

questions.question: What is meant by the “shadowland” of legal discourse that Yenner describes?
options: A secret set of courts operating outside the Constitution
The informal negotiations between lawyers before trial
The artificial conceptual space where institutions must argue in civil‑rights and diversity terms instead of candidly about nature, sex, and their true purposes
The gap between state law and federal law on criminal justice
questions.answer: The artificial conceptual space where institutions must argue in civil‑rights and diversity terms instead of candidly about nature, sex, and their true purposes

questions.question: Which of the following best captures Justice Ginsburg’s core assumption about educational goals for men and women in the VMI case, as analyzed in the lecture?
options: Men and women should be prepared for fundamentally different social destinies in public institutions
Educational goals may legitimately diverge by sex, but only in private schools
If the goals of education are not different for men and women, the means of education should also not significantly differ by sex
Women should receive less rigorous education to preserve traditional roles
questions.answer: If the goals of education are not different for men and women, the means of education should also not significantly differ by sex

questions.question: What was the Virginia Women’s Institute for Leadership (VWIL) created to accomplish in the litigation strategy?
options: To replace VMI entirely as the state’s military college
To provide a women’s program that could be argued to be substantially similar to VMI for equal protection purposes
To train women only for civilian public service careers
To serve as a federal pilot program in gender studies
questions.answer: To provide a women’s program that could be argued to be substantially similar to VMI for equal protection purposes

questions.question: Why, according to Yenner, did VMI quickly diverge from a single, identical physical standard for men and women after coeducation?
options: Because federal law required women to have higher standards than men
Because male cadets demanded lower standards for themselves
Because keeping identical standards would have led to most women washing out, creating legal and political pressure to change them
Because the military prohibited VMI from testing women at all
questions.answer: Because keeping identical standards would have led to most women washing out, creating legal and political pressure to change them

questions.question: What key institutional change at VMI does Yenner highlight as symbolizing the loss of its traditional self‑governing ethos?
options: Replacing the Code of the Gentleman with the administration‑written Code of the Cadet
Adding a new library building
Switching from paper exams to computer‑based testing
Eliminating all physical education requirements
questions.answer: Replacing the Code of the Gentleman with the administration‑written Code of the Cadet

questions.question: What is the significance of footnote 4 in United States v. Carolene Products Co., as discussed in the lecture?
options: It abolished state sovereignty in all economic matters
It announced that the Court would no longer enforce any federal statutes
It signaled a shift toward protecting discrete and insular minorities and fundamental rights under the Equal Protection Clause
It mandated that all military schools become coeducational
questions.answer: It signaled a shift toward protecting discrete and insular minorities and fundamental rights under the Equal Protection Clause

questions.question: How does Yenner characterize the role of anti‑discrimination law in shaping public and legislative discourse?
options: It has no real effect outside courtrooms
It forces policymakers and advocates to hide their real reasons and speak in officially approved categories like diversity and outcomes
It guarantees that all political arguments must be religiously neutral
It encourages more open discussion of natural and theological arguments
questions.answer: It forces policymakers and advocates to hide their real reasons and speak in officially approved categories like diversity and outcomes

questions.question: Why does Yenner argue that some forms of discrimination are necessary for a functioning political community?
options: Because discrimination is always morally good
Because without distinctions such as citizen vs. non‑citizen, institutions like voting and nationhood cannot coherently exist
Because it allows courts to avoid hard cases
Because it ensures permanent advantages for majority groups
questions.answer: Because without distinctions such as citizen vs. non‑citizen, institutions like voting and nationhood cannot coherently exist

questions.question: Which of the following best summarizes the lecture’s critique of Ruth Bader Ginsburg’s brand of feminism?
options: It overvalues traditional domestic roles and undercuts women’s careers
It assumes women can never match men intellectually
It elevates women by making male patterns of life the standard, thereby devaluing distinctively feminine goods and roles
It focuses solely on economic redistribution rather than legal equality
questions.answer: It elevates women by making male patterns of life the standard, thereby devaluing distinctively feminine goods and roles

questions.question: What long‑term strategy does Yenner propose for challenging the VMI precedent in the current legal and political environment?
options: Refusing to comply with any Supreme Court decisions
Abolishing all military academies nationwide
Establishing new all‑male military schools in friendly jurisdictions and using the factual record of VMI’s transformation to press for reconsideration of United States v. Virginia
Amending the Constitution to remove the Equal Protection Clause entirely
questions.answer: Establishing new all‑male military schools in friendly jurisdictions and using the factual record of VMI’s transformation to press for reconsideration of United States v. Virginia
